what_it_does: Scopes a minimal end-to-end front-interface proof slice: it states the goal, draws a hard keep/throw line between company architecture and mockup visuals, lists pre-built pieces to bind, assigns binding seams to members, defines a verified-by-use proof, and sets working discipline.
observations: The build-hold is lifted only for this slice; the full interface stays held until findings complete and Tim judges it.
The goal is one clickable addressed unit rendered in DNA's gallery from data, with clickable sub-addresses that load an ollama-backed Claude Code brain, adapt, and re-render.
A keep/throw line says keep company architecture/address systems, throw away the company mockup visual frontend; look and feel is DNA only.
All listed pieces are asserted as already built: projection Instrument, DNA gallery, fork loadable-brain wire, composition resolver/address spine, wildcard recognition grammar/F14 catalog.
Seams are assigned by member: DNA, fork, projection, composition, wildcard.
The proof is a live round-trip, not documentation or green paint.
Discipline emphasizes investigating actual code, use-don't-build, GPU sequencing, and no assumptions.
standouts: The hard keep/throw line separating structural substrate from throwaway AI-made visuals.
DNA's own 73 reference screens are declared the canonical source of look and feel.
Clicking an element address loads a Claude Code session on an ollama model (kimi-k2.7-code:cloud) bound to that element's context.
The slice is intentionally one addressed state, not the full interface.
concerns: All pieces are asserted 'built' without evidence or definitions inside the file.
Heavy use of project code names assumes shared context not provided here.
'Tim is the only judge of right' centralizes authority without documented criteria or fallback.
conventions: Planning document with owner-scoped seams and explicit success criteria.
Uses internal project names (DNA, fork, projection, composition, wildcard, Instrument, F14).
Markdown headings, bullet lists, and star ratings for emphasis.
References a named decision maker and dated rulings.
notes: A binding plan for the first minimal front-interface slice: keep company addressing, discard mockup visuals, render through DNA's gallery, and wire an ollama-backed brain to element addresses.
questions: What is the exact data schema DNA's gallery expects for a screen?
Is the F14 catalog.json or DNA's own layouts.json the data substrate?
What are the 16 address schemes and which 9 are resolvable?
How is an element's context composed for the Claude Code session?
What are Tim's explicit judgment criteria for lifting the full hold?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file says it is scoping the first slice, and it does so.
apparent_themes: Minimal safe-to-build integration before full expansion
intention_signals: Establish a small, low-risk integration point that proves the front-interface circuit (address -> render -> click -> brain -> mutate -> re-render) works, while refusing to commit to the full interface or to throwaway mockup visuals.
novelty: Explicitly separates a kept structural/addressing substrate from a discarded AI-generated visual frontend, and uses an internal gallery engine's own reference screens as the sole canonical design source.
contribution: Provides a scoped binding contract that preserves the company's addressing and composition architecture, routes all rendering through DNA's data-driven gallery, and attaches an ollama-backed conversational brain at element addresses.
summary_for_embedding: This markdown file scopes the first minimal end-to-end front-interface slice, binding the company's address/composition architecture to DNA's data-driven gallery renderer and an ollama-hosted loadable brain to demonstrate a clickable, element-addressable, adaptive render loop.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to define the minimal provable circuit and member seams for the first build slice while keeping the full interface on hold pending Tim's judgment.
